title: About
text:
I am a junior Data Science and Machine Learning profile with a strong mathematical background and a growing focus on applied machine learning, predictive modeling, and research-oriented problem solving.
I am currently completing an M2 in Data Mathematics after a first master’s degree in Algebra and Applications. My academic path has progressively led me from rigorous mathematical foundations to practical data science, machine learning, deep learning, and Topological Data Analysis (TDA).
My goal is to build robust, interpretable, and useful machine learning systems, whether for business-oriented applications or more advanced scientific problems involving complex and high-dimensional data.











My first master’s degree in Algebra and Applications provided strong foundations in mathematical reasoning, abstraction, and modeling.

















Machine Learning and predictive modeling

Data Science for decision support

Interpretable and reliable ML systems

Topological Data Analysis (TDA)

High-dimensional and biomedical data

Applied research at the intersection of mathematics and machine learning





My current internship focuses on an applied research pipeline combining TDA, ML, and DL for complex biomedical data. In parallel, I develop personal projects in classification, churn prediction, credit risk, and end-to-end ML workflows.
Through this combination of mathematical training, applied projects, and research exposure, I am building a profile aimed at junior roles in Data Science, ML Engineering, and Applied Research Engineering.





clarity in problem formulation

methodological rigor

reproducibility

interpretable results

useful insights for decision-making

bridges between theory and practice





I am currently seeking opportunities aligned with junior roles in:

Data Science

Machine Learning Engineering

Applied Research Engineering

With a long-term interest in building reliable ML systems and contributing to technically demanding, real-world projects.

title: Projects
text:
Objective
Predict SARS-CoV-2 positivity using clinical and biological data through a binary classification workflow.
Approach - Data cleaning and preprocessing
- Feature engineering
- Binary classification with scikit-learn pipelines
- Hyperparameter tuning
- Recall-oriented evaluation to reduce false negatives
Evaluation - Confusion matrix
- Precision / Recall analysis across decision thresholds
- Cross-validation
Key Takeaways - Importance of recall in healthcare-related classification tasks
- Trade-off between precision and sensitivity
- Value of threshold analysis beyond standard accuracy
Illustrative Results
The final evaluation highlights how model assessment must be aligned with the application context. In a medical screening setting, reducing false negatives can be more important than maximizing overall accuracy alone.









These visualizations illustrate the final classification behavior of the model and the threshold-dependent trade-off between precision and recall in a healthcare-oriented setting.
🔗 View Project on GitHub





Objective
Predict customer churn and provide actionable insights to support retention strategies.
Approach - Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
- Feature engineering
- Classification models (baseline + optimized models)
- Model evaluation with business-oriented metrics
- Interpretability whith SHAP ans importance variables
Business Perspective - Identification of high-risk customers
- Analysis of key drivers of churn
- Translation of model outputs into actionable retention strategies
Evaluation - Precision, Recall, F1-score
- Confusion matrix
- ROC / Precision-Recall curves
Key Takeaways - Importance of interpretability in business decision-making
- Trade-off between model performance and actionability
- Value of data-driven insights for customer retention










These visuals highlight the interpretability, threshold optimization, and model comparison process used to translate churn prediction into actionable business insights.
🔗 View Project on GitHub








Default risk prediction

Class imbalance handling

Model calibration and threshold tuning

Interpretability and regulatory perspective






Reproducible ML pipeline

Training → prediction → monitoring

Performance tracking

Data drift detection (planned)






Languages & Tools
Python, Git, Conda
Libraries
pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, matplotlib, seaborn
Core Skills - Data preprocessing and feature engineering
- Model training and evaluation
- Pipeline design
- Cross-validation and tuning
- Model interpretation

title: Research & Internship
text:
Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia (CLL) is a complex and heterogeneous disease with highly variable clinical evolution. Traditional statistical approaches often struggle to capture the underlying structure of high-dimensional biological data such as genomic or transcriptomic signals.
This project is positioned at the intersection of applied mathematics, machine learning, and biomedical data analysis.





The goal of this internship is to develop an integrative analysis pipeline combining Topological Data Analysis (TDA) and Machine Learning (ML) to model relationships between biological markers and clinical outcomes.
The project explores both directions:

Prediction (Biology → Clinical)
Extract robust topological features from biological data and use them to predict disease progression and clinical outcomes.
Inference (Clinical → Biology)
Reconstruct plausible biological representations from observed clinical trajectories using generative and inverse modeling approaches.






Integration of multi-omics and clinical datasets

Construction of topological representations (simplicial complexes, persistence diagrams)

Transformation into vectorized features (persistence images, landscapes)

Machine learning models (random forests, neural networks, kernel methods)

Dimensionality reduction (UMAP, t-SNE)

Exploration of generative and invertible models (VAE, conditional GANs, INNs)





Mathematics - Topological Data Analysis (TDA)
- High-dimensional data analysis
- Dynamical systems (conceptual exposure)
Machine Learning - Representation learning
- Predictive modeling
- Model evaluation and validation
Tools - Python
- scikit-learn
- PyTorch / TensorFlow
- GUDHI, Ripser





Working with complex, high-dimensional, and structured data

Designing hybrid pipelines combining mathematical methods and ML

Understanding the importance of representation in predictive performance

Bridging theoretical concepts with applied machine learning





This work reflects my interest in applied research at the intersection of mathematics and machine learning, especially for complex real-world data.
It complements my portfolio projects, which focus on business-oriented machine learning (churn prediction, risk modeling, ML pipelines), by adding a strong research and modeling dimension.
